title: More Attacks
prose: Mrs Norris turns out not to be the last of it. Colin Creevey, a first-year who never went anywhere without his camera, is found petrified near the same corridor a few weeks later. Then Justin Finch-Fletchley, a Hufflepuff — and Nearly Headless Nick alongside him, which nobody thought possible, since he was already a ghost.

By the time a second wave of attacks hits, the whole school has changed shape around the fear: no one walks anywhere alone if they can help it, Filch patrols the corridors at all hours looking more haunted than usual, and there's a running, half-serious head-count every morning of who's still walking around unpetrified.

Some of the Muggle-born students you know start talking, quietly, about whether their parents might pull them out early. Nobody says it directly, but the shape of the fear is obvious enough: whatever's doing this seems to be choosing its targets.

choices.label: Do what you can to look out for the students most worried.
Keep to yourself and wait for it to pass.
